problems.title: Contains Duplicate
problems.notes: Use a hash set to track seen numbers. O(n) time, O(n) space. Alternative: sort first then check neighbors.

problems.title: Valid Anagram
problems.notes: Count character frequencies in both strings and compare. O(n) time. Can also use sorting for O(n log n).

problems.title: Two Sum
problems.notes: Classic hash map problem. Key insight: complement = target - nums[i]. One-pass or two-pass hash map both work.

problems.title: Group Anagrams
problems.notes: Use sorted string or character count tuple as key in hash map. O(n * k log k) with sorting, O(n * k) with count arrays.

problems.title: Top K Frequent Elements
problems.notes: Count frequencies with hash map, then use min-heap of size k or bucket sort. O(n log k) with heap, O(n) with bucket sort.

problems.title: Valid Sudoku
problems.notes: Use hash sets for rows, columns, and 3x3 boxes. Key format: 'num@row' or use 3 sets per unit. O(1) since board is always 9x9.

problems.title: Product of Array Except Self
problems.notes: Build prefix products and suffix products. O(n) time, O(1) extra space if output array doesn't count. No division allowed.

problems.title: Longest Consecutive Sequence
problems.notes: Use hash set. Only start counting from numbers that don't have a predecessor (num-1 not in set). O(n) time.

problems.title: Valid Palindrome
problems.notes: Two pointers from both ends, skip non-alphanumeric characters. Compare lowercase versions. O(n) time, O(1) space.

problems.title: Two Sum II - Input Array Is Sorted
problems.notes: Sorted array allows two-pointer approach. Left + right sum vs target determines which pointer to move. O(n) time, O(1) space.

problems.title: 3Sum
problems.notes: Sort first, then fix one number and use two-sum on the rest. Skip duplicates at each level. O(n²) time.

problems.title: Container With Most Water
problems.notes: Two pointers at both ends. Move the pointer with smaller height inward. O(n) time. Area = width * min(height[left], height[right]).

problems.title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
problems.notes: Track minimum price so far, calculate max profit at each day. Kadane's-like single pass. O(n) time, O(1) space.

problems.title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
problems.notes: Expand window with right pointer, contract from left when duplicate found. Use hash set or hash map for character indices. O(n) time.

problems.title: Longest Repeating Character Replacement
problems.notes: Sliding window with max frequency count. Window is valid if (window size - max frequency) <= k. O(n) time with 26-char array.

problems.title: Valid Parentheses
problems.notes: Use a stack. Push opening brackets, pop and match when closing bracket encountered. Check stack is empty at end. O(n) time.

problems.title: Min Stack
problems.notes: Two stacks: one for values, one for mins. Or store pairs (val, currentMin). getMin() in O(1), push/pop in O(1).

problems.title: Daily Temperatures
problems.notes: Monotonic decreasing stack storing indices. When current temp > stack top, pop and calculate days difference. O(n) time.

problems.title: Binary Search
problems.notes: Classic binary search template. While left <= right, calculate mid, compare with target, adjust boundaries. O(log n).

problems.title: Find Minimum in Rotated Sorted Array
problems.notes: Modified binary search. Compare mid with right: if mid > right, min is in right half; else min is at mid or left half. O(log n).

problems.title: Search in Rotated Sorted Array
problems.notes: Modified binary search. Determine which half is sorted by comparing mid with left/right. Check if target lies in sorted half. O(log n).

problems.title: Reverse Linked List
problems.notes: Iterative: prev, curr, next pointers. Recursive: reverse rest then attach head. Both O(n) time, iterative uses O(1) space.

problems.title: Merge Two Sorted Lists
problems.notes: Dummy head + two pointers. Compare nodes and link smaller one. Handle remaining nodes. Recursive solution also possible. O(n+m) time.

problems.title: Reorder List
problems.notes: Three steps: find middle (slow/fast pointers), reverse second half, merge two halves alternately. O(n) time, O(1) space.

problems.title: Remove Nth Node From End of List
problems.notes: Fast pointer moves n steps ahead, then both move together. When fast reaches end, slow is at node before target. O(n) one-pass.

problems.title: Linked List Cycle
problems.notes: Floyd's Cycle Detection (slow/fast pointers). If they meet, cycle exists. Can also use hash set but O(n) space. O(n) time, O(1) space.

problems.title: LRU Cache
problems.notes: Hash map + Doubly Linked List. Map stores key -> node reference. List maintains usage order. get/put in O(1). Move accessed nodes to front.

problems.title: Invert Binary Tree
problems.notes: Swap left and right children recursively (DFS) or iteratively (BFS with queue). O(n) time. Classic recursive tree problem.

problems.title: Maximum Depth of Binary Tree
problems.notes: Recursive DFS: max depth = 1 + max(left depth, right depth). BFS level-order also works counting levels. O(n) time.

problems.title: Diameter of Binary Tree
problems.notes: Post-order DFS. At each node, diameter through node = left height + right height. Track global max. Return height to parent. O(n).

problems.title: Balanced Binary Tree
problems.notes: Bottom-up DFS. Return -1 if subtree unbalanced, else return height. Balanced if |left - right| <= 1. O(n) time.

problems.title: Same Tree
problems.notes: Recursive DFS: compare current nodes, then left subtrees, then right subtrees. BFS with queue also works. O(n) time.

problems.title: Subtree of Another Tree
problems.notes: For each node in main tree, check if subtree matches starting there (sameTree helper). O(m * n) worst case, can optimize with hashing.

problems.title: Lowest Common Ancestor of a Binary Search Tree
problems.notes: Leverage BST property. If both p,q < root, go left. If both > root, go right. Else root is LCA. O(h) time where h = height.

problems.title: Binary Tree Level Order Traversal
problems.notes: BFS with queue. Process nodes level by level, tracking level size. DFS with level parameter also works. O(n) time.

problems.title: Binary Tree Right Side View
problems.notes: BFS: last node of each level. DFS: pre-order but visit right child first, track max depth seen. O(n) time.

problems.title: Count Good Nodes in Binary Tree
problems.notes: DFS carrying max value seen so far from root. If current >= max, it's good. Update max and recurse. O(n) time.

problems.title: Validate Binary Search Tree
problems.notes: DFS with min/max bounds. Each node must be within (min, max) range. Left child max = node.val, right child min = node.val. O(n).

problems.title: Kth Smallest Element in a BST
problems.notes: In-order traversal gives sorted order. Count nodes until kth. Can also use iterative stack. O(h + k) time, O(h) space.

problems.title: Kth Largest Element in a Stream
problems.notes: Min-heap of size k. Top of heap is kth largest. Add new element: if larger than min, pop and push. O(log k) per add.

problems.title: Last Stone Weight
problems.notes: Max-heap (use negative values in JS min-heap). Pop two heaviest, smash, push difference back if non-zero. O(n log n).

problems.title: Kth Largest Element in an Array
problems.notes: Min-heap of size k: O(n log k). Quickselect: O(n) average. Sorting: O(n log n). All valid approaches.

problems.title: Number of Islands
problems.notes: Grid DFS/BFS. Mark visited cells (change to '0' or use visited set). Count connected components. O(m * n) time.

problems.title: Max Area of Island
problems.notes: Similar to Number of Islands but track area of each island. DFS returns area count. Track global max. O(m * n).

problems.title: Clone Graph
problems.notes: Hash map: original node -> clone. DFS or BFS traversal. For each neighbor, recursively clone if not in map. O(V + E).

problems.title: Pacific Atlantic Water Flow
problems.notes: Reverse thinking: DFS from ocean borders inward. Track cells reachable from Pacific and Atlantic separately. Intersection is answer. O(m * n).

problems.title: Surrounded Regions
problems.notes: DFS from border 'O's to mark unsurrounded regions. Then flip remaining 'O's to 'X' and marked ones back to 'O'. O(m * n).

problems.title: Course Schedule
problems.notes: Detect cycle in directed graph. DFS with three states: unvisited, visiting, visited. Or use Kahn's algorithm (indegree/BFS). O(V + E).

problems.title: Course Schedule II
problems.notes: Topological sort. DFS post-order reversed, or Kahn's BFS with indegree. Return empty if cycle detected. O(V + E).
